evals.prompt: I want to build a FastAPI backend with PostgreSQL, JWT authentication, and a REST API. I'm a solo developer and this will be a production app. Set up my project.
evals.expected_output: Creates Python project structure (src/routers, models, schemas, services), CLAUDE.md with FastAPI/Python context, .mcp.json with postgres and context7 MCP servers, .claude/settings.json with ruff hooks and .env protection, .env.example with DATABASE_URL and JWT_SECRET, recommends feature-dev and commit-commands plugins.

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md exists and mentions FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and JWT
.mcp.json includes a postgres or supabase MCP server
.mcp.json includes context7 MCP server
.claude/settings.json includes a Python linting/formatting hook (ruff or similar)
.claude/settings.json includes a hook to protect .env files from edits
.env.example exists and includes DATABASE_URL and a JWT-related variable
Project folder structure includes src/ directory with at least two of: routers/, models/, schemas/, services/

evals.prompt: Help me start a Claude agent app. I want it to be able to search the web, read files, and call external APIs. I'm building this for a small startup team.
evals.expected_output: Creates Python agent app structure (src/agent.py, tools/, prompts/), CLAUDE.md explaining agent architecture, .mcp.json with brave-search and context7 MCP servers, recommends claude-api skill and agent-sdk-dev plugin, .claude/settings.json with ruff hooks, offers playground concept map visualization of agent architecture.

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md exists and mentions agent, Claude API, or Anthropic SDK
.mcp.json includes a web search MCP server (brave-search or similar)
.mcp.json includes context7 MCP server
Folder structure includes src/tools/ or src/agent.py, reflecting an agent architecture
.env.example includes ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
An AI/agent-relevant plugin is mentioned in summary (agent-sdk-dev, claude-api, or mcp-server-dev)

evals.prompt: I need to set up a Next.js e-commerce site with Stripe payments, Supabase for the database, and Clerk for auth. This is for a small team of 3 developers.
evals.expected_output: Creates Next.js app router structure (src/app, components, lib, types), CLAUDE.md with Next.js/Supabase/Stripe/Clerk tech stack details, .mcp.json with supabase, stripe, context7, and playwright MCP servers, .claude/settings.json with ESLint+Prettier hooks, recommends commit-commands and pr-review-toolkit plugins, .env.example with NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, CLERK_SECRET_KEY.

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md mentions Next.js, Stripe, Supabase, and Clerk
.mcp.json includes supabase MCP server
.mcp.json includes stripe MCP server
.mcp.json includes context7 MCP server
.claude/settings.json includes ESLint or Prettier hook for TypeScript/JavaScript
.env.example includes variables for Supabase (URL or key), Stripe (secret key), and Clerk (secret key)
Folder structure includes src/app/ directory (Next.js App Router pattern)
A team-collaboration plugin is mentioned (pr-review-toolkit, commit-commands, or code-review)
